question: Will the price of Ethereum be above $2,300 on April 17?

fragility_points: Overnight crypto-wide risk-off move before noon ET.
key_assumptions: ETH stays in roughly its current short-run trading regime through noon ET on April 17.
No Binance-specific anomaly distorts the settlement candle.
main_logical_chain: Current Binance ETHUSDT pricing is above 2300, so the base rate starts with Yes favored.
Recent Binance closes mostly above 2300 support continuation rather than a break below the threshold.
But next-day crypto volatility and one-minute settlement mechanics reduce confidence versus the market price.
payload.main_thesis: ETH above 2300 at the Binance noon ET minute on April 17 is somewhat more likely than not, but less likely than the market implies because one-minute settlement fragility keeps No live.
quote_anchors: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for ETH/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone
payload.source_quality_view: Good source quality: contract text plus Binance documentation/endpoints are strong, but evidence independence is only medium because Binance is the underlying source for both mechanics and price context.
strongest_disconfirmers: The contract settles on one specific one-minute candle, so an ordinary sub-2% move lower could still resolve No.
strongest_supports: Binance ETHUSDT traded around 2335.94 on April 16, above the 2300 threshold.

unresolved_ambiguities: Polymarket points to the Binance chart UI as the resolution surface, while candle mechanics were additionally checked through Binance API docs.
payload.what_would_change_view: A move below 2300 ahead of settlement, a sharp crypto selloff, or evidence of Binance settlement-candle anomalies would move me toward No or a coinflip.

fragility_points: Short-horizon crypto volatility can erase a $30 buffer quickly.
